summary: Linked `itkirbycutterbeam.c` by moving it from NonMatching to Matching and defining its `ItemStateTable` data, while also filling in several previously stubbed or partially typed item functions for Ness yoyo and Ice Climbers string. The author described this as matching some missed item files from the prior PR. The decomp.dev report credited 8 new matches, including `it_802C0010`, `it_802BF180`, `itNessyoyo_UnkMotion1_Phys`, `itNessyoyo_UnkMotion2_Phys`, `fn_802C28DC`, `fn_802C29E8`, `fn_802C2AF4`, and `itkirbycutterbeam` `.data`, plus 10 improvements in still-unmatched item code.
what_changed: `configure.py` changed `melee/it/items/itkirbycutterbeam.c` from `Object(NonMatching, ...)` to `Object(Matching, ...)`, making the PR title's link target explicit.

`itkirbycutterbeam.c` removed placeholder/platform/type includes that were no longer needed and added `ItemStateTable it_803F6798[]` with the motion callbacks `itKirbycutterbeam_UnkMotion0_Anim`, `Phys`, and `Coll`; decomp.dev reported the unit's `.data` became a new 56-byte 100% match.

`itclimbersstring.c` replaced several forward-only placeholder declarations with real implementations for string/link update routines including `fn_802C28DC`, `fn_802C29E8`, `fn_802C2AF4`, `it_802C2CA8`, `it_802C2DB0`, `it_802C32D4`, and `itClimbersstring_UnkMotion3_Anim`.
`itclimbersstring.h` gained prototypes for the newly exposed link/string helpers, including signatures using `ItemLink`, `Vec3`, `itClimbersStringAttributes`, `Item`, and `f32`.

`itCharItems.h` refined `itClimbersStringAttributes`: the old `pad_00[0x18]` was split into `pad_00[0x8]`, `f32 x8`, `pad_0C[0x8]`, and `f32 x14`, enabling code to reference string segment spacing/physics fields instead of raw padding.

`itnessyoyo.c` filled in multiple yoyo physics/animation/link routines, including `itNessyoyo_UnkMotion1_Phys`, `itNessyoyo_UnkMotion2_Phys`, `itNessyoyo_UnkMotion3_Anim`, `it_802BF030`, `it_802BF180`, `it_802BF800`, `it_802BF900`, `it_802BFEC4`, and `it_802C0010`.

`itnessyoyo.h` replaced several `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` or loose `void*` declarations with typed prototypes involving `ItemLink`, `Vec3`, `itYoyoAttributes`, `Item_GObj`, `Item`, `s32`, `f32`, and `bool`.
smart_moves: The PR used typed struct fields to unlock matching: `itClimbersStringAttributes` gained concrete `f32 x8` and `f32 x14` members exactly where the new climbers string code reads segment length/gravity-like values.

The new yoyo and climbers string code follows a repeated item-link pattern: derive a world position from `HSD_JObjSetupMatrix` plus `PSMTXIdentity`/`PSMTXConcat`, copy translation components into `Vec3`, then update `ItemLink` chains and item display state.

Cleanup helpers were kept local as `static inline` in the C files, preserving ownership cleanup behavior while likely helping codegen for `itNessyoyo_UnkMotion3_Anim` and `itClimbersstring_UnkMotion3_Anim`.

The PR did not overclaim full unit completion: the bot report shows several yoyo/climbers functions improved but still below 100%, while the exact `itkirbycutterbeam.c` file was safely promoted to Matching.
Header cleanup was paired with implementation work, so future callsites can use typed prototypes instead of `UNK_PARAMS` for yoyo link/collision helpers.
decomp_lessons: For item chain mechanics such as yoyo, climbers string, hookshot-like links, and grapple-like item vars, compare sibling implementations: this PR includes `it/items/itlinkhookshot.h` in both yoyo and climbers string files, suggesting shared `ItemLink` behavior was useful evidence.

When matching item physics that read JObj transforms, the exact sequence of `PSMTXIdentity`, setting translation slots, `HSD_JObjSetupMatrix`, `PSMTXConcat`, and copying `m[0][3..]` into a `Vec3` can be important for instruction order and stack shape.
Replacing padding with named-but-still-unknown fields such as `x8` and `x14` is a useful intermediate step: it records real offsets and types without inventing semantic names not yet proven.

Action-range checks against `ftLib_80086C0C(owner)` are a recurring cleanup guard for spawned fighter articles; here Ness yoyo checks actions `0x156..0x15B`, while climbers string checks `0x15B..0x164`.

The decomp.dev match report is valuable evidence for triage: it separated exact new matches from near-matches, helping future work focus on `it_802BF900`, `itNessyoyo_UnkMotion3_Anim`, `it_802BFEC4`, `it_802BF030`, `it_802BF800`, and climbers string helpers that remained imperfect.
naming_conventions: Unidentified functions kept address-based names like `it_802BF030`, `it_802C2CA8`, and `fn_802C28DC`, matching the project's convention when behavior is not yet confidently named.
Item motion callback names follow the item-prefixed pattern, e.g. `itNessyoyo_UnkMotion1_Phys`, `itNessyoyo_UnkMotion3_Anim`, and `itKirbycutterbeam_UnkMotion0_Coll`.
Struct fields that are typed but not semantically identified remain offset-style names such as `itClimbersStringAttributes.x8`, `x14`, and item-var fields like `xDD4_itemVar.samusgrapple.x8`.
Header address comments were preserved and expanded, e.g. `/* 2BF030 */`, `/* 2C2CA8 */`, and `/* 2C32D4 */`, keeping symbol lookup searchable by ROM address.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used small stack padding arrays such as `f32 pad[1]`, `pad_top`, `pad_mid`, and `pad_bottom`, plus `PAD_STACK(8)`, indicating stack-layout tuning for MWERKS/Gekko matching.
Explicitly initialized matrix translation components to `0.0f` or `it_804DD150` before JObj matrix concatenation to mirror original constant usage and instruction order.
Kept nested scopes around temporaries in cleanup and distance calculations, which can affect lifetime, stack reuse, and generated code shape.
Used direct bit checks and boolean flags on `ItemLink` collision/link state (`x2C_b0`, `x2C_b1`, `x2C_b2`) rather than abstracting them away, preserving low-level control flow for matching.
For `it_802BF900`, built translation and scale matrices manually and set JObj flags `JOBJ_USER_DEF_MTX | JOBJ_MTX_INDEP_PARENT | JOBJ_MTX_INDEP_SRT`, matching low-level rendering state manipulation.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
The decomp.dev bot reported measurable progress: matched code +1908 bytes, linked code +2588 bytes, matched data +56 bytes, linked data +136 bytes.

Bot-listed exact new matches were `it_802C0010`, `it_802BF180`, `fn_802C28DC`, `fn_802C29E8`, `itNessyoyo_UnkMotion1_Phys`, `itNessyoyo_UnkMotion2_Phys`, `fn_802C2AF4`, and `itkirbycutterbeam` `.data`.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that finish the near-matches in `src/melee/it/items/itnessyoyo.c`, especially `it_802BF900`, `itNessyoyo_UnkMotion3_Anim`, `it_802BFEC4`, `it_802BF030`, and `it_802BF800`.
Find later PRs that replace `itClimbersStringAttributes.x8` and `x14` with semantic field names.
Search for other item chain implementations using `ItemLink`, `itlinkhookshot.h`, `PSMTXConcat`, and `HSD_JObjSetupMatrix` as matching references.
Check whether the static function declarations added to `itclimbersstring.h` were later moved back to C files or kept for matching/header ordering reasons.
